bio: Major General Mohammad Pakpour was an [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) career officer who served as Commander of the IRGC Ground Forces from 2014 before being elevated to IRGC Commander-in-Chief following the death of [Hossein Salami](/people/hossein-salami) in June 2025. A veteran of the [Iran–Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war), Pakpour commanded cross-border IRGC operations against Kurdish opposition groups in Iraq and was closely associated with proxy force coordination. As IRGC Commander-in-Chief he oversaw the organisation during the highly degraded post-Twelve-Day War period, attempting to reconstitute command structures decimated by Israeli strikes. He was killed on 28 February 2026 in Israeli airstrikes as part of a sustained campaign targeting Iran's military leadership. His appointment had been confirmed by [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei).

sources.title: Iran military leadership killed in February strikes

editorial_notes: Born year approximate. IRGC Ground Forces commander 2014–2025, then IRGC C-in-C until killed Feb 28 2026.
